diagrams.title: Graphics System Architecture
diagrams.description: A block diagram showing the fundamental components of a computer graphics system, including input devices, processing units, memory, and output display. This helps students understand how hardware and software components work together to generate and display graphics.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the hardware and software architecture of computer graphics systems, showing data flow from input to final display output

diagrams.title: Graphics Rendering Pipeline
diagrams.description: A flowchart illustrating the stages of the 3D graphics rendering pipeline from geometric modeling to final pixel display. This visualizes how 3D scenes are transformed and rendered into 2D images.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the step-by-step process of converting 3D scene descriptions into rendered 2D images, a fundamental concept in real-time graphics

diagrams.title: Graphics Application Domains
diagrams.description: A mind map or categorized diagram showing the various application areas of computer graphics across different industries and fields. This helps students appreciate the wide-ranging real-world applications.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the diverse practical applications of computer graphics technology across multiple industries and domains
